SOX11 Is Regulated by EGFR-STAT3 and Promotes Epithelial–Mesenchymal Transition in Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma

evidence_entities.text: EGF supplement significantly upregulates both the protein and mRNA expression of the Sox11 gene
EGFR inhibitor suppresses the expression of SOX11 in HNSCC cells
STAT3 binds to the Sox11 gene promoter and transcriptionally upregulates the expression of Sox11 in HNSCC cells
SOX11 upregulates EMT transcription factors, vimentin, fibronectin, and N-cadherin while downregulating E-cadherin
SOX11 binds to the Twist1 gene promoter and regulates TWIST1 expression
SOX11 promotes tumor growth in vivo in xenograft mouse model

notes: This study demonstrates a regulatory pathway where EGF-EGFR-STAT3 signaling upregulates SOX11, which then promotes epithelial-mesenchymal transition in head and neck squamous cell carcinoma
The research includes both in vitro cell culture experiments and in vivo xenograft mouse models
Multiple EMT transcription factors are shown to be regulated by SOX11, establishing it as a key regulator in the EMT process
